questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An LCR series circuit with $100 \Omega$ resistance is connected to an AC source of 200 V and angular frequency $300 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$. When only capacitance is removed, the current lags behind the voltage by $60^{\circ}$. When only the inductance is removed the current leads the voltage by $60^{\circ}$. Calculate the power dissipated in watt in the LCR circuit.
questions.solution: From the given data $\omega L=\frac{1}{\omega C}$. Thus power factor is zero Power dissipated $=\frac{V^2}{R}=400 \mathrm{~W}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The inductor in a L-C oscillation has a maximum potential difference of 16 V and maximum energy of $160 \mu \mathrm{~J}$. Find the value of capacitor in pF in $\mathrm{L}-\mathrm{C}$ circuit.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In an AC circuit capacitive reactance of capacitor is $X_C=50 \sqrt{2} \Omega$ and inductive reactance of inductor is $X_L=50 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{ohm}$. If the source voltage is 100 volts. Then find the current passing through the source.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The circuit shown in figure is in the steady state with switch $\mathrm{S}_1$ closed and $S_2$ to open. At $t=0, S_1$ is opened and $S_2$ is closed. The first instant $t$, when the energy in the inductor becomes one-third of that in the capacitor $\mathrm{C}_2$ is $\frac{\pi}{\alpha}$ millisecond. Find the value of $\alpha$.
questions.solution:
$\omega=\frac{1}{\sqrt{L C}}=\frac{1}{2 \times 10^{-5}}$

Equation of oscillation of charge on capacitor is $\mathrm{q}=\mathrm{q}_0 \cos \omega \mathrm{t}$
Energy in capacitor $=\frac{q^2}{2 C}$ and energy in inductor $=\frac{1}{2} L i^2$
Given : $\frac{1}{2} L i^2=\frac{1}{3} \frac{q^2}{2 C} ;$ solving $t=\frac{\pi}{300} \mathrm{~ms} \Rightarrow \alpha=300$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
